# GETTING-STARTED | @keetanetwork/keetanet-client

*   [GETTING-STARTED](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html)
    

KeetaNet Client SDK Getting Started[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html#keetanet-client-sdk-getting-started)

================================================================================================================================================

Documentation[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html#documentation)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

See the full documentation for the KeetaNet Client JavaScript/TypeScript SDK at [https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/)
.

Installation[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html#installation)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The KeetaNet Client JavaScript/TypeScript SDK can be loaded with:

    $ npm install @keetanetwork/keetanet-client
    

Concepts[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html#concepts)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Accounts[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html#accounts)

[Accounts](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html)
 in the KeetaNet SDK are the way in which key pairs or identifiers are represented.

For key pairs, they securely store the private key (where applicable) and provide operations like [digital signing](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html#sign)
 and [verification](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html#verify)
, [key derivation from seed](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html#fromseed)
 and [passphrase](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html#seedfrompassphrase)
, and [encryption](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html#encrypt)
 and [decryption](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html#decrypt)
 using ECIES.

For identifiers, they are used to represent the identity of a specific kind of address, such as a Token, the Network Address, or a Storage account.

Additionally, for key pair accounts the key may be held internally or externally using an external key provider. No particular external key provider interface (e.g. PKCS#11) is specified but users may implement their own provider using the [ExternalKeyPair](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.ExternalKeyPair.html)
 interface.

### Blocks[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html#blocks)

[Blocks](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
 are used within KeetaNet to represent a collection of operations (transactions) by an account. They contain the [account identifier](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html#account)
, the [network identifier](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html#network)
, the [previous block identifier](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html#previous)
, an optional [signer](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html#signer)
 (if different from the account), a [timestamp](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html#date)
, and a list of [operations](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html#operations)
.

The block is digitally signed by the signer (if present, otherwise the account) and the signature is included in the block. The block is identified by its [hash](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html#hash-1)
 which is the hash of the block not including the signature.

The KeetaNet SDK also provides a block builder method which allows the user to create blocks in an incremental fashion. The block builder is created using the [BlockBuilder](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BlockBuilder.html)
 class, however in most cases a [UserClientBuilder](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientBuilder.html)
 from a [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#initbuilder)
 should be used because it will handle things like getting the correct network and previous block hash.

### Operations[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html#operations)

[Operations](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BlockOperation.html)
 describe the actions performed by an account on the ledger. They are fundamentally composed of effects, which are the specific changes or constraints performed on the ledger.

An example operation is a [Send](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BlockOperationSEND.html)
 operation which has the effects of decrementing the balance of the sender, incrementing the balance of the receiver, and validating that the sender's balance does not drop below zero.

The KeetaNet SDK provides [a number of operations](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BlockOperation.html)
 which are used to perform actions on the ledger.

### Votes[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html#votes)

[Votes](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Vote.html)
 in KeetaNet are used for consensus forming among "representatives". Each vote is an assertion by a representative that a specific group of blocks (identified by their hashes) are valid and conform to the rules of the ledger of that representative.

Votes are used to form a consensus on the state of the ledger and are aggregated by the representatives to form a "vote staple". A vote staple is a collection of votes and the blocks which are referenced by those vote.

Votes come in two flavors: temporary and permanent. Temporary votes are used to get initial consensus on a set of blocks. A quorum of temporary votes can be "traded in" for permanent votes.

When a temporary vote is issued by a representative the vote and block are stored on the representative's "side ledger", which is a non-synchronized portion of the ledger. The client can request a copy of the block from the representative [Client.getPendingBlock](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#getpendingblock)
 or [UserClient.pendingBlock](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#pendingblock)
 method, or the [Client.getVoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#getvotestaple)
 method to with the "`side`" parameter set to `side` to retrieve the vote staple from the representative's side ledger.

Additionally, the [UserClient.recover](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#recover)
 method can be used to query all known representatives for side ledger vote staples to complete any partially completed consensus rounds.

### Vote Staples[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html#vote-staples)

[Vote Staples](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
, as discussed above, are a collection of votes and the blocks. Every vote in a vote staple must be for the same set of blocks in the same order.

Vote Staples are the unit of transaction in the KeetaNet network. The set of blocks in a vote staple are applied altogether as a single atomic transaction acting on the ledger.

### Networks[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html#networks)

[Networks](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/variables/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.src_config.networksArray.html)
 in KeetaNet a group of representative nodes and clients which all agree to a common set of rules and initial ledger state. There can be many different networks, such as the "test" network, "main" network, and "dev" network. Each network has its own set of representatives and clients.

The network is identified within each block by the [network identifier](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html#network)
 property, which is an integer.

Each network also has a [Network Address](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#networkaddress)
, which is a special kind of account derived from the network identifier. The purpose of the network address is to define permissions which apply to the network as a whole, such as the permission to create tokens, or create storage accounts.

### Tokens[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html#tokens)

[Tokens](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#token)
 in KeetaNet are a special kind of account which is used to represent a fungible asset. Tokens can be created using the [Create Identifier](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BlockOperationCREATE_IDENTIFIER.html)
 method though the [UserClientBuilder.generateIdentifier](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientBuilder.html#generateidentifier)
 method is recommended.

Each network also has a base token which is used for consensus and other management functions. The base token can be thought of as the native currency of the network and is derived from the network identifier.

### Ledger[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html#ledger)

The ledger within KeetaNet refers to a couple of different things:

*   The live state of accounts and their balances of tokens, as well as other attributes
*   The record of how that state came to be, represented by vote staples

#### The ledger state[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html#the-ledger-state)

The ledger state is the current state of all accounts and their balances of tokens, it can be thought of like this:

| Account | Base Token | Token 1 | Token 2 | Token 3 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| kta1 | 1   | 100 | 200 | 300 |
| kta2 | 5   |     | 0   | 3   |
| kta3 |     | 90  |     |     |

Where `kta1`, `kta2`, and `kta3` are the account identifiers, and the columns represent the balance of that account for the base token and other tokens.

#### The ledger history[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html#the-ledger-history)

The ledger history is the record of how the ledger state came to be. It is represented by the set of vote staples which have been applied to the ledger.

This is expressed in two different ways in the KeetaNet SDK

*   The [UserClient.history](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#history)
     method which returns a list of [vote staples](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
     which have affected the given account.
*   The [UserClient.chain](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#chain)
     method which returns a list of [blocks](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
     which have been applied for a given account.

These two differ in that the history method returns all vote staples which affected an account, even if they were not issued by the account -- for example if a transfer was made to the account, the history method would return the vote staple which included the transfer, but the chain method would not because it was not issued by the account.

Additionally there is a method to [filter a list of vote staples](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#filterstapleoperations-2)
 to a list of operations which are relevant to a specific account. This is useful because the list of operations in a vote staple may include changes that are uninteresting from an account perspective.

### Permissions[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html#permissions)

[Permissions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Permissions.html)
 in KeetaNet are used to allow users and operators to manage access to resources on the network. They are used to control who can create tokens, create storage accounts, as well as delegate permissions to other accounts.

There are two kinds of permissions:

*   [Base](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BaseSet.html)
     permissions are the permissions which are defined by the network. These are the permissions which the network representatives will look at to grant or deny access
*   [External](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.ExternalSet.html)
     permissions are the permissions which applications can define. These are the permissions which are defined by the users of KeetaNet and the network representatives will not process them. These are useful for applications which want to define their own permissions and in the future may be used with Smart Contracts.

Additionally, permissions may be applied to a specific account or set as default permissions on some the following resources:

*   Network Account: `ACCESS`, `STORAGE_CREATE`, `TOKEN_ADMIN_CREATE`
*   Token Accounts: `ACCESS`
*   Storage Accounts: `ACCESS`, `STORAGE_CAN_HOLD`, `STORAGE_DEPOSIT`

Setting default permissions is done with the [UserClient.setInfo](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#setinfo)
 method. Setting permission on an account are set with the [UserClient.updatePermissions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#updatepermissions)
 method.

#### Base Permissions[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html#base-permissions)

The base permissions are defined by the network and are used to control access to the network. Each base permission has a [flag name](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/enums/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BaseFlag.html)
 which can be used to identify the permission. The base permissions are:

*   `ACCESS` - The permission to access the resource it is granted on -- if this permission is missing then no access to the resource is granted, this is useful as to create deny-list or permit-list resources.
*   `ADMIN` - The `ADMIN` permission allows the `principal` to perform all actions on the resource, excluding deleting the resource and transferring ownership. For example, administrators of Token resources can mint and burn tokens and set the permissions of the token.
*   `OWNER` - The owner permission grants all the privileges of the `ADMIN` permission but also the ability to delete the resource and transfer ownership. All resources have exactly 1 owner.
*   `UPDATE_INFO` - The update info permission allows the `principal` to update the information of the resource. See [UserClient.setInfo](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#setinfo)
     for more information on how to set the info.
*   `STORAGE_CREATE` - This permission on the network account allows the specified account to create storage accounts. As a default permission it allows everyone to create storage accounts.
*   `TOKEN_ADMIN_CREATE` - This permission on the network account allows the specified account to create token accounts. As a default permission it allows everyone to create token accounts.
*   `TOKEN_ADMIN_SUPPLY` - This permission on a token account allows the specified specified account to mint and burn tokens. This is useful for fungible tokens where the supply can be changed.
*   `TOKEN_ADMIN_MODIFY_BALANCE` - This permission on a token account allows the specified specified account to modify the balance of holders of this token's balance.
*   `STORAGE_DEPOSIT` - This permission on a storage account allows the specified specified account to deposit the specified tokens into the storage account.
*   `STORAGE_CAN_HOLD` - This permission on a storage account allows the specified specified account to hold the specified token in the storage account.
*   `SEND_ON_BEHALF` - This permission of an account allows the specified principal account to send the specified token on behalf of the account. This is useful for shared accounts where multiple accounts can send on behalf of the account. To use this feature see the [signer](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientConfig.html#signer)
     option to specify a different signatory for the transaction from the specified [account](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientConfig.html#account)
    .
*   `PERMISSION_DELEGATE_ADD` - This permission grants a subset of the abilities of the current account to some other account. Must be used with [AdjustMethod.ADD](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#add)
    .
*   `PERMISSION_DELEGATE_REMOVE` - This permission revokes a subset of the abilities that have been delegated. Must be used with [AdjustMethod.SUBTRACT](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#subtract)
    
*   `MANAGE_CERTIFICATE` - This permission allows the specified account to manage the certificate of the account. This is used to set the certificate of the account, which is used to verify the identity of the account.

### Clients[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html#clients)

[Clients](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html)
 in the KeetaNet SDK are the ways in which applications primarily interact with the KeetaNet network. There are two different clients supported:

*   [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html)
     is the main client which is used to interact with the network. It is used to send transactions, query the ledger, and perform other operations through the [UserClientBuilder](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientBuilder.html)
    .
*   [Client](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html)
     is a lower level client which is used to by the [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html)
     to interact with the network.

Usage[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html#usage)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The KeetaNet SDK is designed to be used in a variety of environments, including NodeJS, the browser, and other JavaScript environments. It has TypeScript type definitions.

### NodeJS[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html#nodejs)

The KeetaNet SDK can be used in NodeJS by importing the package:

    import * as KeetaNet from '@keetanetwork/keetanet-client';
    

From there, the SDK can be used to create a [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html)
 and interact with the KeetaNet network. See the [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html)
 documentation for more information on how to use the client.

Many other functions needed to support usage of the SDK are also in the `@keetanetwork/keetanet-client` package, such as the [Account](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html)
 class, which is used to represent key pairs and identifiers, and the [Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
 class, which is used to represent blocks on the ledger -- these are all imported with the `KeetaNet` import and accessed with the [KeetaNetSDK.lib](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/variables/KeetaNetSDK.lib.html)
 namespace.

### Browser[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html#browser)

The KeetaNet SDK can be used in the browser by including the script tag:

    <script src="https://static.network.keeta.com/keetanet-browser.js"></script>
    

The KeetaNet SDK can then be accessed using the `KeetaNet` global variable.

Examples[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html#examples)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Basic usage (NodeJS)[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html#basic-usage-nodejs)

The [KeetaNetSDK.UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html)
 is the main entry point to interact with the KeetaNet network. It is initialized with a network name and an account using the [KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.fromNetwork](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#fromnetwork)
 constructor.

    import * as KeetaNet from '@keetanetwork/keetanet-client';const seed = KeetaNet.lib.Account.generateRandomSeed({ asString: true });const account = KeetaNet.lib.Account.fromSeed(seed, 0);const client = KeetaNet.UserClient.fromNetwork('test', account);async function main() {	console.debug(await client.chain());}main().then(function() {	process.exit(0);}, function(error) {	console.error(error);	process.exit(1);});
    

### Basic usage (Browser)[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html#basic-usage-browser)

The SDK can be loaded in the browser with a script tag:

    <html>	<head>		<script src="https://static.network.keeta.com/keetanet-browser.js"></script>	</head>	<body>		<script>			const seed = KeetaNet.lib.Account.generateRandomSeed({ asString: true });			const account = KeetaNet.lib.Account.fromSeed(seed, 0);			const client = KeetaNet.UserClient.fromNetwork('test', account);			client.chain().then(console.debug);		</script>	</body></html>
    

### Full examples[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html#full-examples)

*   [Basic Example](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/media/index.js)
    
*   [Real World Assets with NFT](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/media/index-1.js)

*   External

# KeetaNetSDK | @keetanetwork/keetanet-client

*   [KeetaNetSDK](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.html)
    

Module KeetaNetSDK
==================

JavaScript and TypeScript SDK for the KeetaNet network.

This SDK provides a low- and high-level interfaces to the KeetaNet network.

See the [Getting Started](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/documents/GETTING-STARTED.html)
 guide for an overview of how to get started with the SDK.

-------

# Client | @keetanetwork/keetanet-client

*   [KeetaNetSDK](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.html)
    
*   [Client](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html)
    

Class Client
============

The Client class provides a low-level interface to the KeetaNet network. It handles sending messages to the KeetaNet representatives and parsing the responses.

It does not handle any of the higher-level logic of the KeetaNet network, for that there is the [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html)
 class.

#### Example[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#example)

      import { Client } from '@keetanetwork/keetanet-client';  const client = Client.fromNetwork('test');  const blocks = await client.getChain('keeta_...');
    

##### Index

[Cconstructor](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#constructor)

Constructors
------------

### constructor[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#constructor)

*   new Client(reps: [ClientRepresentative](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.ClientRepresentative.html)
    \[\]): [Client](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html)
    [](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#constructorclient)
    
    Create a new instance of the [Client](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#)
     class from the given set of representatives. This is used to create a new instance of the [Client](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#)
     class with a custom set of representatives.
    
    New instances should be cleaned up with the [destroy](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#destroy)
    () method when they are no longer needed.
    
    In general this is not needed and the [Client.fromNetwork](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#fromnetwork)
    () method should be used instead.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   reps: [ClientRepresentative](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.ClientRepresentative.html)
        \[\]
        
        The representatives to use for this instance of the [Client](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#)
         class
        
        *   ##### `Optional`weight?: bigint
            
            The weight of the representative, in terms of voting power
            
    
    #### Returns [Client](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html)
    
    A new instance of the [Client](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#)
     class
    

Properties
----------

### destroyed[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#destroyed)

destroyed: boolean = false

Indication of whether or not this client has been destroyed.

### logger[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#logger)

logger: Pick<Console, "log" | "warn" | "error"\> = Client.DefaultLogger

The logger to use for this instance of the [Client](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#)
 class. This is defined by the `Client.DefaultLogger` property, but can be overridden by the application.

### `Readonly`stats[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#stats)

stats: { api: { called: number; failures: number } } = ...

Stats for this instance of the client

#### Type declaration

*   ##### api: { called: number; failures: number }
    
    Breakdown of the API calls made
    
    *   ##### called: number
        
        The total number of times the API was called
        
    *   ##### failures: number
        
        The number of times the API failed
        

### `Static` `Readonly`Builder[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#builder)

Builder: typeof [UserClientBuilder](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientBuilder.html)
 = UserClientBuilder

A reference to the [UserClientBuilder](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientBuilder.html)
 class, which is the high-level interface to building blocks.

### `Static` `Readonly`Config[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#config)

Config: [src/config](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.src_config.html)
 = Config

A reference to the [Config](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.src_config.html)
 class, which provides access to the KeetaNet configuration utilities.

### `Static`DefaultLogger[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#defaultlogger)

DefaultLogger: Pick<Console, "log" | "warn" | "error"\> = console

The default logger to use for new instances of the [Client](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#)
 class. This is set to the `console` object by default, but can be changed by the application.

### `Static`isInstance[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#isinstance)

isInstance: (obj: any, strict?: boolean) \=> obj is [Client](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html)
 = ...

Check if the given object is an instance of the [Client](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#)
 class. This is preferred to using the `instanceof` operator because it will work across different contexts.

Methods
-------

### \[asyncDispose\][](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#asyncdispose)

*   "\[asyncDispose\]"(): Promise<void\>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#asyncdispose-1)
    
    This enables the use of `using` to automatically clean up the instance of the [Client](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#)
     class when it is no longer needed.
    
    It calls the [destroy](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#destroy)
    () method to clean up the instance when it goes out of scope.
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>
    

### computeBuilderBlocks[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#computebuilderblocks)

*   computeBuilderBlocks(  
        network: bigint,  
        builder: [UserClientBuilder](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientBuilder.html)
    ,  
    ): Promise<[ComputeBlocksResponse](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.ComputeBlocksResponse.html)
    \>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#computebuilderblocks-1)
    
    Compute the blocks for a given builder. This will take the pending operations and compute the blocks for them.
    
    This will additionally mutate the state of the builder to "seal" the blocks meaning new operations will be added to new blocks.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   network: bigint
        
        The network to use for the builder
        
    *   builder: [UserClientBuilder](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientBuilder.html)
        
        The builder to use for the computation
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<[ComputeBlocksResponse](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.ComputeBlocksResponse.html)
    \>
    
    The blocks that were computed
    

### destroy[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#destroy)

*   destroy(): Promise<void\>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#destroy-1)
    
    Destroy this instance of the [Client](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#)
     class. This will clean up any resources used by the instance and stop any background tasks.
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>
    

### getAccountHeadInfo[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#getaccountheadinfo)

*   getAccountHeadInfo(  
        account: string | [GenericAccount](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GenericAccount.html)
    ,  
        rep?: [src/config](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.src_config.html)
    .[Representative](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.src_config.Representative.html)
     | "ANY",  
    ): Promise<null | { block: [Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
    ; height: bigint }\>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#getaccountheadinfo-1)
    
    Get the current head block for a given account. This will return the entire block, or null if the account has not created any blocks.
    
    An account with no blocks may still have a balance because other users may have sent tokens to it.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   account: string | [GenericAccount](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GenericAccount.html)
        
        The account to get the head block for
        
    *   rep: [src/config](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.src_config.html)
        .[Representative](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.src_config.Representative.html)
         | "ANY" = 'ANY'
        
        The representative to get the head block from -- this is generally "ANY" in which case the best representative will be used, but it is possible to request a specific representative
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<null | { block: [Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
    ; height: bigint }\>
    
    The head block and height for the account or null if the account has not created any blocks
    

### getAccountInfo[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#getaccountinfo)

*   getAccountInfo(  
        account: string | [GenericAccount](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GenericAccount.html)
    ,  
        rep?: [ClientRepresentative](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.ClientRepresentative.html)
     | "ANY",  
    ): Promise<[GetAccountStateAPIResponseFormatted](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GetAccountStateAPIResponseFormatted.html)
    \>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#getaccountinfo-1)

### getBlock[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#getblock)

*   getBlock(blockhash: string | [BlockHash](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BlockHash.html)
    ): Promise<null | [Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
    \>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#getblock-1)
    
    Get a block from the specified representative by its block hash.
    
    The default mode of operation will request the block from the main ledger of the "best" representative.
    
    The "side" ledger is a special ledger that is used to hold unpublished blocks that the given representative has learned about but which have not been published to the network. This is used as part of the voting process.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   blockhash: string | [BlockHash](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BlockHash.html)
        
        The block hash to get the block for
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<null | [Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
    \>
    
    The block for the given block hash or null if the block does not exist on the given ledger
    
*   getBlock(  
        blockhash: string | [BlockHash](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BlockHash.html)
    ,  
        side?: [LedgerSelector](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.LedgerSelector.html)
    ,  
        rep?: [ClientRepresentative](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.ClientRepresentative.html)
     | "ANY",  
    ): Promise<null | [Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
    \>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#getblock-2)
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   blockhash: string | [BlockHash](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BlockHash.html)
        
        The block hash to get the block for
        
    *   `Optional`side: [LedgerSelector](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.LedgerSelector.html)
        
        The side of the ledger to get the block from -- this is generally "main", but it is possible to request "side" ledger blocks
        
    *   `Optional`rep: [ClientRepresentative](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.ClientRepresentative.html)
         | "ANY"
        
        The representative to get the block from -- this is generally "ANY" in which case the best representative will be used, but it is possible to request a specific representative
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<null | [Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
    \>
    
    The block for the given block hash or null if the block does not exist on the given ledger

*   getCertificateByHash(  
        account: string | [GenericAccount](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GenericAccount.html)
    ,  
        certificateHash: string | [CertificateHash](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.CertificateHash.html)

    \>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#getcertificatebyhash-1)

    #### Parameters

        The account to get the certificate for

        The hash of the certificate to get

### getChain[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#getchain)

*   getChain(  
        account: string | [GenericAccount](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GenericAccount.html)
    ,  
        options?: {  
            depth?: number;  
            endBlock?: string | [BlockHash](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BlockHash.html)
    ;  
            startBlock?: string | [BlockHash](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BlockHash.html)
    ;  
        },  
    ): Promise<[Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
    \[\]\>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#getchain-1)
    
    Get the chain for a given account. This is the set of blocks that the account has created. This will return the blocks in reverse order, with the most recent block first.
    
    This is a paginated request and may return only a partial set of blocks for a given request if there are more blocks to be fetched from the representative. The `startBlock` parameter can be used to fetch the next set of blocks. Once all blocks have been fetched an empty array will be returned.
    
    The `depth` parameter can be used to limit the number of blocks returned. The default is to leave it up to the representative to determine the number of blocks to return. The representative may return fewer than the requested number of blocks even if there are more blocks available, except that it will always return at least 1 block if there are any blocks available.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   account: string | [GenericAccount](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GenericAccount.html)
        
        The account to get the chain for
        
    *   options: {  
            depth?: number;  
            endBlock?: string | [BlockHash](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BlockHash.html)
        ;  
            startBlock?: string | [BlockHash](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BlockHash.html)
        ;  
        } = {}
        
        The options to use for the request
        
        *   ##### `Optional`depth?: number
            
            The maximum number of blocks to return -- this is used to limit the number of blocks returned
            
        *   ##### `Optional`endBlock?: string | [BlockHash](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BlockHash.html)
            
            The block hash to stop on -- this is used to limit the chain to a specific range of blocks
            
        *   ##### `Optional`startBlock?: string | [BlockHash](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BlockHash.html)
            
            The block hash to start from -- this is used to paginate the request
            
    
    #### Returns Promise<[Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
    \[\]\>
    
    The chain of blocks for the given account, in reverse order starting with the most recent block
    

### getHeadBlock[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#getheadblock)

*   getHeadBlock(  
        account: string | [GenericAccount](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GenericAccount.html)
    ,  
        rep?: [src/config](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.src_config.html)
    .[Representative](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.src_config.Representative.html)
     | "ANY",  
    ): Promise<null | [Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
    \>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#getheadblock-1)
    
    Get the current head block for a given account. This will return the entire block, or null if the account has not created any blocks.
    
    An account with no blocks may still have a balance because other users may have sent tokens to it.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   account: string | [GenericAccount](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GenericAccount.html)
        
        The account to get the head block for
        
    *   rep: [src/config](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.src_config.html)
        .[Representative](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.src_config.Representative.html)
         | "ANY" = 'ANY'
        
        The representative to get the head block from -- this is generally "ANY" in which case the best representative will be used, but it is possible to request a specific representative
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<null | [Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
    \>
    
    The head block for the account or null if the account has not created any blocks
    

### getHistory[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#gethistory)

*   getHistory(  
        account: null | string | [GenericAccount](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GenericAccount.html)
    ,  
        options?: { depth?: number; startBlocksHash?: string | [VoteBlockHash](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteBlockHash.html)
     },  
    ): Promise<{ voteStaple: [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
     }\[\]\>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#gethistory-1)
    
    Get the history for a given account. This is the set of vote staples that have interacted with the account. This is different from the chain in that it includes vote staples that have affected this account but are not directly created by this account.
    
    This is a paginated request and may return only a partial set of vote staples for a given request if there are more vote staples to be fetched from the representative. The `startBlocksHash` parameter can be used to fetch the next set of vote staples. It can be computed from the last entry in the previous response's `blocksHash` field. Once all vote staples have been fetched an empty array will be returned.
    
    The `depth` parameter can be used to limit the number of vote staples returned. The default is to leave it up to the representative to determine the number of vote staples to return. The representative may return fewer than the requested number of vote staples even if there are more vote staples available, except that it will always return at least 1 vote staple if there are any vote staples available.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   account: null | string | [GenericAccount](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GenericAccount.html)
        
        The account to get the history for -- if null then the history for all accounts will be returned
        
    *   options: { depth?: number; startBlocksHash?: string | [VoteBlockHash](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteBlockHash.html)
         } = {}
        
        The options to use for the request
        
        *   ##### `Optional`depth?: number
            
            The maximum number of vote staples to return -- this is used to limit the number of vote staples returned
            
        *   ##### `Optional`startBlocksHash?: string | [VoteBlockHash](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteBlockHash.html)
            
            The block hash to start from -- this is used to paginate the request
            
    
    #### Returns Promise<{ voteStaple: [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
     }\[\]\>
    
    The history of vote staples for the given account, in reverse order starting with the most recent vote staple

### getPendingBlock[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#getpendingblock)

*   getPendingBlock(account: [GenericAccount](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GenericAccount.html)
    ): Promise<null | [Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
    \>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#getpendingblock-1)
    
    Get the pending block for a given account. This will return the block on the representative's side ledger that is pending vote completion. This is used to recover the pending block for an account that has not been published yet.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   account: [GenericAccount](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GenericAccount.html)
        
        The account to get the pending block for
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<null | [Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
    \>
    
    The pending block for the account or null if there is no pending block

### getVoteStaple[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#getvotestaple)

*   getVoteStaple(blockhash: string | [BlockHash](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BlockHash.html)
    ): Promise<null | [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
    \>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#getvotestaple-1)
    
    Get the vote staple for a given block hash. This will return the entire vote staple including the blocks whose hash has not been requested.
    
    The default mode of operation will request the block from the main ledger of the "best" representative.
    
    The "side" ledger is a special ledger that is used to hold unpublished votes and blocks that the given representative has learned about but which have not been published to the network. This is used as part of the voting process.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   blockhash: string | [BlockHash](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BlockHash.html)
        
        The block hash to get the vote staple for
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<null | [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
    \>
    
    The vote staple for the given block hash or null if the vote staple does not exist on the given ledger
    
*   getVoteStaple(  
        blockhash: string | [BlockHash](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BlockHash.html)
    ,  
        side?: [LedgerStorage](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.LedgerStorage.html)
    ,  
        rep?: [src/config](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.src_config.html)
    .[Representative](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.src_config.Representative.html)
    ,  
    ): Promise<null | [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
    \>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#getvotestaple-2)
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   blockhash: string | [BlockHash](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BlockHash.html)
        
        The block hash to get the vote staple for
        
    *   `Optional`side: [LedgerStorage](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.LedgerStorage.html)
        
        The side of the ledger to get the vote staple from -- this is generally "main", but it is possible to request "side" ledger blocks
        
    *   `Optional`rep: [src/config](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.src_config.html)
        .[Representative](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.src_config.Representative.html)
        
        The representative to get the staple from -- this is generally "ANY" in which case the best representative will be used, but it is possible to request a specific representative
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<null | [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
    \>
    
    The vote staple for the given block hash or null if the vote staple does not exist on the given ledger
    

### getVoteStaplesAfter[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#getvotestaplesafter)

*   getVoteStaplesAfter(  
        moment: Date,  
        limit?: number,  
        bloomFilter?: string,  
    ): Promise<[VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
    \[\]\>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#getvotestaplesafter-1)
    
    Get a list of vote staples that have been added to the representative's ledger since a given moment in time.
    
    This method is used to bootstrap a new node with the vote staples that have been added to the ledger since the last time the node was updated.
    
    It is a paginated request and may return only a partial set of vote staples for a given request if there are more vote staples to be fetched from the representative. The `moment` parameter can be used to fetch the next set of vote staples, and the `bloomFilter` parameter can be used to tell the server not to include any duplicate vote staples that have already been sent as part of the last page in the case of a `moment` overlap. Once all vote staples have been fetched an empty array will be returned.
    
    The bloom filter is constructed from the bytes of the vote staples to exclude.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   moment: Date
        
        The moment in time to get the vote staples after
        
    *   `Optional`limit: number
        
        The maximum number of vote staples to return
        
    *   `Optional`bloomFilter: string
        
        The bloom filter to use to filter the vote staples which have already been sent
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<[VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
    \[\]\>
    
    The list of vote staples that have been added to the representative's ledger since the given moment in time

### transmit[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#transmit)

*   transmit(  
        blocks: [Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
    \[\],  
        options?: [PublishOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PublishOptions.html)
    ,  
    ): Promise<{ from: "direct"; publish: boolean; voteStaple: [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
     }\>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#transmit-1)
    
    Transmit a set of blocks to the network. This will request short votes and permanent votes for the blocks and then publish them to the network. Optionally it will generate a fee block from a user provided function if fees are required.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   blocks: [Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
        \[\]
        
        The blocks to transmit
        
    *   `Optional`options: [PublishOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PublishOptions.html)
        
        User provided options [PublishOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PublishOptions.html)
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<{ from: "direct"; publish: boolean; voteStaple: [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
     }\>
    
    The result of the publish operation
    

### transmitBuilder[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#transmitbuilder)

*   transmitBuilder(  
        builder: [UserClientBuilder](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientBuilder.html)
    ,  
        network: bigint,  
        options?: [PublishOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PublishOptions.html)
    ,  
    ): Promise<{ from: "direct"; publish: boolean; voteStaple: [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
     }\>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#transmitbuilder-1)
    
    Transmit a set of blocks to the network. This will request short votes and permanent votes for the blocks and then publish them to the network.
    
    The `blocks` builder will be computed using [computeBuilderBlocks](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#computebuilderblocks)
     and then transmitted.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   builder: [UserClientBuilder](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientBuilder.html)
        
        The UserClientBuilder to compute and transmit transmit
        
    *   network: bigint
        
        The network to use for the builder (if using a builder)
        
    *   `Optional`options: [PublishOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PublishOptions.html)
        
        User provided options [PublishOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PublishOptions.html)
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<{ from: "direct"; publish: boolean; voteStaple: [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
     }\>
    
    The result of the publish operation
    

### transmitStaple[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#transmitstaple)

*   transmitStaple(  
        votesAndBlocks: [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
    ,  
        reps?: [ClientRepresentative](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.ClientRepresentative.html)
    \[\],  
    ): Promise<{ from: "direct"; publish: boolean; voteStaple: [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
     }\>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#transmitstaple-1)
    
    Publish a Vote Staple to the network. This will publish the votes and blocks to the network.
    
    If `reps` is not defined then the highest weight representative will be used. If `reps` is defined then the votes and blocks will be published to all representatives in the list.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   votesAndBlocks: [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
        
        The votes and blocks to publish
        
    *   `Optional`reps: [ClientRepresentative](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.ClientRepresentative.html)
        \[\]
        
        The representatives to publish to
        
        *   ##### `Optional`weight?: bigint
            
            The weight of the representative, in terms of voting power
            
    
    #### Returns Promise<{ from: "direct"; publish: boolean; voteStaple: [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
     }\>
    
    The result of the publish operation as well as the vote staple
    

### updateReps[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#updatereps)

*   updateReps(addNewReps?: boolean): Promise<void\>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#updatereps-1)
    
    Update this client's view of the network representatives. This will fetch the list of representatives from the network and update the list of representatives that this client will use.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   addNewReps: boolean = false
        
        If true, add any new representatives to the list of representatives
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>
    

### `Static`fromNetwork[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#fromnetwork)

*   fromNetwork(network: "main" | "staging" | "test" | "dev"): [Client](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html)
    [](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#fromnetwork-1)
    
    Construct a new instance of the [Client](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#)
     class from the given network name. This will use the default representatives for the network based on the configuration.
    
    New instances should be cleaned up with the [destroy](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#destroy)
    () method when they are no longer needed.
    
    This is the recommended way to create a new instance of the [Client](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#)
     class.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   network: "main" | "staging" | "test" | "dev"
        
        The network to use for this instance of the [Client](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#)
         class
        
    
    #### Returns [Client](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html)
    
    A new instance of the [Client](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html#)
     class

# UserClient | @keetanetwork/keetanet-client

*   [KeetaNetSDK](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.html)
    
*   [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html)
    

Class UserClient
================

The UserClient class provides a high-level interface, user-oriented interface to the Keeta network. It is designed to be easy to use and handle the most common cases for applications building on top of the Keeta network.

#### Example[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#example)

      import { UserClient } from '@keetanetwork/keetanet-client';  import { lib as KeetaNetLib } from '@keetanetwork/keetanet-client`;  const seed = '...'; // 64 character hex seed  const account = KeetaNetLib.Account.fromSeed(seed, 0);  const client = UserClient.fromNetwork('test');  const blocks = await client.chain();

Constructors

### constructor[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#constructor)

*   new UserClient(config: [UserClientConfig](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientConfig.html)
    ): [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html)
    [](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#constructoruserclient)
    
    Construct a new instance of the [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#)
     class from the given configuration. This will use the default representatives for the network based on the configuration.
    
    New instances should be cleaned up with the [destroy](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#destroy)
     method when they are no longer needed.
    
    The recommended way to create a new instance of the [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#)
     class is to use the [fromNetwork](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#fromnetwork)
     method.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   config: [UserClientConfig](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientConfig.html)
        
    
    #### Returns [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html)

### signer[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#signer)

*   get signer(): null | [Account](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html)
    <[ECDSA\_SECP256K1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256k1)
     | [ED25519](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ed25519)
     | [ECDSA\_SECP256R1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256r1)
    \>
    
    Get the signer for this instance, if it was set. This is the account that will be used to sign blocks and transactions. If this is null, then the client is readonly and will not be able to publish blocks or transactions.
    
    #### Returns null | [Account](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html)
    <[ECDSA\_SECP256K1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256k1)
     | [ED25519](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ed25519)
     | [ECDSA\_SECP256R1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256r1)
    \>

### \[asyncDispose\][](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#asyncdispose)

*   "\[asyncDispose\]"(): Promise<void\>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#asyncdispose-1)
    
    This enables the use of `using` to automatically clean up the instance of the [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#)
     class when it is no longer needed.
    
    It calls the [destroy](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#destroy)
    () method to clean up the instance when it goes out of scope.
    
    #### Returns Promise<void\>

### initializeNetwork[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#initializenetwork)

*   initializeNetwork(  
        initOpts: {  
            addSupplyAmount: bigint;  
            baseNetworkInfo?: Partial<  
                Pick<  
                    [AccountInfo](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.AccountInfo.html)
    ,  
                    "name"  
                    | "description"  
                    | "metadata"  
                    | "defaultPermission",  
                \>,  
            \>;  
            baseTokenInfo?: [BaseTokenInfo](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BaseTokenInfo.html)
    ;  
            delegateTo?: [Account](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html)
    <[ECDSA\_SECP256K1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256k1)
     | [ED25519](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ed25519)
     | [ECDSA\_SECP256R1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256r1)
    \>;  
            voteSerial?: bigint;  
        },  
        options?: [UserClientOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html)
    ,  
    ): Promise<{ from: "direct"; publish: boolean; voteStaple: [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
     }\>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#initializenetwork-1)
    
    Generate and publish the blocks needed to initialize a new network with the given parameters.
    
    This is generally only needed once per network and is used to initialize the network with a base token and a representative in order to start-up the network.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   initOpts: {  
            addSupplyAmount: bigint;  
            baseNetworkInfo?: Partial<  
                Pick<  
                    [AccountInfo](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.AccountInfo.html)
        ,  
                    "name"  
                    | "description"  
                    | "metadata"  
                    | "defaultPermission",  
                \>,  
            \>;  
            baseTokenInfo?: [BaseTokenInfo](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BaseTokenInfo.html)
        ;  
            delegateTo?: [Account](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html)
        <[ECDSA\_SECP256K1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256k1)
         | [ED25519](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ed25519)
         | [ECDSA\_SECP256R1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256r1)
        \>;  
            voteSerial?: bigint;  
        }
        
        The options to use for the initialization
        
        *   ##### addSupplyAmount: bigint
            
            The amount of supply to add to the network of the base token
            
        *   ##### `Optional`baseNetworkInfo?: Partial<  
                Pick<  
                    [AccountInfo](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.AccountInfo.html)
            ,  
                    "name"  
                    | "description"  
                    | "metadata"  
                    | "defaultPermission",  
                \>,  
            \>
            
        *   ##### `Optional`baseTokenInfo?: [BaseTokenInfo](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BaseTokenInfo.html)
            
        *   ##### `Optional`delegateTo?: [Account](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html)
            <[ECDSA\_SECP256K1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256k1)
             | [ED25519](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ed25519)
             | [ECDSA\_SECP256R1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256r1)
            \>
            
            The representative account to delegate the supply to
            
        *   ##### `Optional`voteSerial?: bigint
            
            The serial number to use for the vote -- must never be reused by the representative
            
    *   options: [UserClientOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html)
         = {}
        
        The options to use for the request
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<{ from: "direct"; publish: boolean; voteStaple: [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
     }\>
    
    The vote staple that was generated and whether it was able to be published

    \[\]; from: "publish-aid"; publish: boolean },  
    \>

        method: [SUBTRACT](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#subtract)

    \[\]; from: "publish-aid"; publish: boolean },

    #### Parameters

         | [CertificateHash](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.CertificateHash.html)

         or a [CertificateHash](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.CertificateHash.html)
         instance.

### modTokenSupplyAndBalance[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#modtokensupplyandbalance)

*   modTokenSupplyAndBalance(  
        amount: bigint,  
        token: [TokenAddress](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.TokenAddress.html)
    ,  
        options?: [UserClientOptionsReadOnly](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptionsReadOnly.html)
    ,  
    ): Promise<  
        | { from: "direct"; publish: boolean; voteStaple: [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
     }  
        | { blocks: [Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
    \[\]; from: "publish-aid"; publish: boolean },  
    \>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#modtokensupplyandbalance-1)
    
    Modify both the token supply (mint/burn) and account balance for a given account of a given token.
    
    This will mint if a positive amount is given and burn if a negative amount is given, and the add or subtract the same amount from the account balance.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   amount: bigint
        
        The amount to add or subtract from the token supply and account balance
        
    *   token: [TokenAddress](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.TokenAddress.html)
        
        The token to modify the supply and balance for
        
    *   options: [UserClientOptionsReadOnly](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptionsReadOnly.html)
         = {}
        
        The options to use for the request
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<  
        | { from: "direct"; publish: boolean; voteStaple: [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
     }  
        | { blocks: [Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
    \[\]; from: "publish-aid"; publish: boolean },  
    \>
    
    The vote staple that was generated and whether it was able to be published

     extends "change"\>(

        \]

### publishBuilder[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#publishbuilder)

*   publishBuilder(  
        builder: [UserClientBuilder](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientBuilder.html)
    ,  
        options?: [PublishOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PublishOptions.html)
    ,  
    ): Promise<  
        | { from: "direct"; publish: boolean; voteStaple: [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
     }  
        | { blocks: [Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
    \[\]; from: "publish-aid"; publish: boolean },  
    \>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#publishbuilder-1)
    
    Compute the blocks needed to publish a given builder and then publish those blocks to the network. The builder should generally not be used after this method is called as the blocks will be published and the builder will be in an invalid state.
    
    In general, the [UserClientBuilder.publish](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientBuilder.html#publish)
     method should be used instead of this one.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   builder: [UserClientBuilder](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientBuilder.html)
        
        The builder to publish
        
    *   options: [PublishOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PublishOptions.html)
         = {}
        
        options for publishing [PublishOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PublishOptions.html)
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<  
        | { from: "direct"; publish: boolean; voteStaple: [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
     }  
        | { blocks: [Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
    \[\]; from: "publish-aid"; publish: boolean },  
    \>
    
    The vote staple that was generated and whether it was able to be published

*   send(  
        to: string | [GenericAccount](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GenericAccount.html)
    ,  
        amount: number | bigint,  
        token: string | [TokenAddress](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.TokenAddress.html)

     }

    \[\]; from: "publish-aid"; publish: boolean },  
    \>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#send-1)
    
    Send some tokens from this account to another account.
    
    If an `external` identifier is provided, it will be included in the Send operation and can be used by the recipient to identify the transaction.

    *   `Optional`external: string

    *   options: [UserClientOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html)
         = {}
        
        The options to use for the request
        
    *   retries: number = 0
        
        The number of times the request has been retried
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<  
        | { from: "direct"; publish: boolean; voteStaple: [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
     }  
        | { blocks: [Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
    \[\]; from: "publish-aid"; publish: boolean },  
    \>

     }\>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#transmit-1)

        \[\]

     }\>

    }[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#filterstapleoperations-3)
