### `Static`fromNetwork[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#fromnetwork)

*   fromNetwork(  
        network: "main" | "staging" | "test" | "dev",  
        signer: null | [Account](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html)
    <[ECDSA\_SECP256K1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256k1)
     | [ED25519](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ed25519)
     | [ECDSA\_SECP256R1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256r1)
    \>,  
        options?: [UserClientOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html)
    ,  
    ): [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html)
    [](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#fromnetwork-1)
    
    Construct a new instance of the [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#)
     class from the given network name. This will use the default representatives for the network based on the configuration.
    
    New instances should be cleaned up with the [destroy](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#destroy)
    () method when they are no longer needed.
    
    This is the recommended way to create a new instance of the [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#)
     class.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   network: "main" | "staging" | "test" | "dev"
        
        The network to use for this instance of the [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#)
         class
        
    *   signer: null | [Account](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html)
        <[ECDSA\_SECP256K1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256k1)
         | [ED25519](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ed25519)
         | [ECDSA\_SECP256R1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256r1)
        \>
        
        The account to use for this instance of the [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#)
         class, or null if this is a read-only client
        
    *   `Optional`options: [UserClientOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html)
        
        The options to use for this instance of the [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#)
         class
        
        *   ##### `Optional`account?: [GenericAccount](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GenericAccount.html)
            
            The account to use for the user client, if this is not supplied then the `signer` will be used (if available) otherwise this there will be no account and some operations may not be available.
            
        *   ##### `Optional`generateFeeBlock?: (staple: [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
            , options?: [UserClientOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html)
            ) \=> Promise<[Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
            \>
            
            Client provided function to compute the fee block associated with any transactions
            
        *   ##### `Optional`publishAidURL?: string
            
            If using the publish aid (see [\['usePublishAid'\]](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html)
            ) the URL to use.
            
            The default depends on the network configuration.
            
        *   ##### `Optional`usePublishAid?: boolean
            
            Use the "publish aid" service to publish blocks.
            
            This service handles requesting short votes and permanent votes and publishing to the network. This can reduce latency for clients far away from representatives because they only need to wait for a single response instead of multiple round-trips.
            
            The default is false.
            
    
    #### Returns [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html)
    
    A new instance of the [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#)
     class
    

### `Static`fromSimpleSingleRep[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#fromsimplesinglerep)

*   fromSimpleSingleRep(  
        hostname: string,  
        ssl: boolean,  
        repKey: string | [Account](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html)
    <[ECDSA\_SECP256K1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256k1)
     | [ED25519](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ed25519)
     | [ECDSA\_SECP256R1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256r1)
    \>,  
        networkID: bigint,  
        networkAlias: "main" | "staging" | "test" | "dev",  
        signer: null | [Account](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html)
    <[ECDSA\_SECP256K1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256k1)
     | [ED25519](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ed25519)
     | [ECDSA\_SECP256R1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256r1)
    \>,  
        options?: [UserClientOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html)
    ,  
    ): [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html)
    [](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#fromsimplesinglerep-1)
    
    Create an instance of the UserClient class from a specific representative. This will use the given representative to initialize the connection to the network, however it will find other representatives to use for the network as well.
    
    This is useful for testing and development purposes, but in general it is recommended to use the [fromNetwork](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#fromnetwork)
     method to create a new instance of the UserClient class.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   hostname: string
    *   ssl: boolean
    *   repKey: string | [Account](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html)
        <[ECDSA\_SECP256K1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256k1)
         | [ED25519](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ed25519)
         | [ECDSA\_SECP256R1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256r1)
        \>
    *   networkID: bigint
    *   networkAlias: "main" | "staging" | "test" | "dev"
    *   signer: null | [Account](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html)
        <[ECDSA\_SECP256K1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256k1)
         | [ED25519](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ed25519)
         | [ECDSA\_SECP256R1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256r1)
        \>
    *   `Optional`options: [UserClientOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html)
        
    
    #### Returns [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html)
    

### `Static`getConfigFromNetwork[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#getconfigfromnetwork)

*   getConfigFromNetwork(  
        network: "main" | "staging" | "test" | "dev",  
        options?: [UserClientOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html)
    ,  
    ): Omit<[UserClientConfig](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientConfig.html)
    , "signer"\>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#getconfigfromnetwork-1)
    
    A helper method to get the configuration from the network alias and options. This will use the default representatives for the network based on the [configuration](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.src_config.html)
    .
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   network: "main" | "staging" | "test" | "dev"
        
        The [network](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.src_config.Networks.html)
         to use to generate the configuration
        
    *   `Optional`options: [UserClientOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html)
        
        The [options](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html)
         to use to generate the configuration
        
    
    #### Returns Omit<[UserClientConfig](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientConfig.html)
    , "signer"\>
    
    The configuration object for the [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html#)
     class

# Account | @keetanetwork/keetanet-client

*   [KeetaNetSDK](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.html)
    
*   [Referenced](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html)
    
*   [Account](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html)
    

Class Account<T>
================

Account class, which is used to represent a key pair or an identifier account (which have no private key) such as tokens.

#### Type Parameters

*   T extends [AccountKeyAlgorithm](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/enums/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.AccountKeyAlgorithm.html)
     = Exclude<[AccountKeyAlgorithm](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/enums/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.AccountKeyAlgorithm.html)
    , [IdentifierKeyAlgorithm](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.IdentifierKeyAlgorithm.html)
    \>
    
    The type of the key algorithm used for this account.

# Block | @keetanetwork/keetanet-client

Class Block
===========

Block: An item which contains a number of operations (transactions) which originated from an account at a particular instant

#### Implements

*   Omit<[BlockV2Canonical](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BlockV2Canonical.html)
    , "version"\>

### hash[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html#hash-1)

*   get hash(): [BlockHash](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.BlockHash.html)
    
    Hash of the block minus the signature
    
    XXX:TODO: Should the hash of the block normally include the signature ? One reason against is that it would allow for identical blocks that only differ by signature (which isn't signed)

# Vote | @keetanetwork/keetanet-client

*   [KeetaNetSDK](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.html)
    
*   [Referenced](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html)
    
*   [Vote](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Vote.html)
    

Class Vote
==========

A vote is a certificate issued indicating that the issuer "vouches" for the blocks specified will fit into the ledger of the operator/issuer.

#### Hierarchy ([View Summary](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/hierarchy.html#KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Vote)
, Expand)

*   [PossiblyExpiredVote](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PossiblyExpiredVote.html)
    *   Vote

### timestamp[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html#timestamp)

        preferRep?: [Account](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html)
    <[ECDSA\_SECP256K1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256k1)
     | [ED25519](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ed25519)
     | [ECDSA\_SECP256R1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256r1)
    \>,  
    ): Date[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html#timestamp-1)
    
    Get the timestamp of the staple
    
    This is the average of the timestamps of the votes, unless a particular account is specified then that timestamp is used if it issued a vote in the staple.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   `Optional`preferRep: [Account](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html)
        <[ECDSA\_SECP256K1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256k1)
         | [ED25519](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ed25519)
         | [ECDSA\_SECP256R1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256r1)
        \>
    
    #### Returns Date

### `Static`fromVotesAndBlocksWithFiltering[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html#fromvotesandblockswithfiltering)

*   fromVotesAndBlocksWithFiltering<[T](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html#fromvotesandblockswithfilteringt)
     extends [VoteBlockBundle](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteBlockBundle.html)
    \>(  
        this: [VoteBundleConstructor](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteBundleConstructor.html)
    <[T](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html#fromvotesandblockswithfilteringt)
    \>,  
        votes: [PossiblyExpiredVote](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PossiblyExpiredVote.html)
    \[\],  
        blocks: [Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
    \[\],  
        opts: undefined | [VoteOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteOptions.html)
    ,  
    ): null | [T](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html#fromvotesandblockswithfilteringt)
    [](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html#fromvotesandblockswithfiltering-1)
    
    Convert a list of Votes and Blocks into a VoteStaple This is slightly different from VoteStaple.fromVotesAndBlocks in that it will filter the supplied votes to only include those that are permanent if any permanent votes are present, otherwise only temporary votes will be included
    
    Additionally, it will filter out any votes that are expired
    
    #### Type Parameters
    
    *   T extends [VoteBlockBundle](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteBlockBundle.html)
        
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   this: [VoteBundleConstructor](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteBundleConstructor.html)
        <[T](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html#fromvotesandblockswithfilteringt)
        \>
    *   votes: [PossiblyExpiredVote](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PossiblyExpiredVote.html)
        \[\]
    *   blocks: [Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
        \[\]
    *   opts: undefined | [VoteOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteOptions.html)
        
    
    #### Returns null | [T](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html#fromvotesandblockswithfilteringt)

### `Static`Legacy[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Log.html#legacy)

*   Legacy(name?: string): [Log](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Log.html)
    [](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Log.html#legacy-1)
    
    The legacy logger
    
    This is a singleton instance of the logger that registers a console target that emits logs immediately to the console.
    
    This also sets the log level based on the `<name>_DEBUG` environment variable, if available and adds filtering based on the `<name>_DEBUG_FILTER` environment variable, if available.
    
    The default value for `<name>` is `KEETANET`.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   name: string = 'KEETANET'
    
    #### Returns [Log](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Log.html)

Properties
----------

### account[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GetAccountStateAPIResponseFormatted.html#account)

account: [GenericAccount](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GenericAccount.html)

The account for which this information is for

### balances[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GetAccountStateAPIResponseFormatted.html#balances)

balances: [GetAllBalancesResponse](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GetAllBalancesResponse.html)

The balances for the account Each token will have a balance, even if it is 0 This will be an empty array if the account is not open

#### Type declaration

*   ##### balance: bigint
    
    The account balance of the specified token
    
    This is in raw units, and not the display units that the token uses
    
*   ##### token: [TokenAddress](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.TokenAddress.html)
    
    The account of the token
    

### currentHeadBlock[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GetAccountStateAPIResponseFormatted.html#currentheadblock)

currentHeadBlock: string | null

The current head block for the account, if there is one this will be null if the account is not open

### currentHeadBlockHeight[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GetAccountStateAPIResponseFormatted.html#currentheadblockheight)

currentHeadBlockHeight: string | null

The current height of the head block for the account, if there is one this will be null if the account is not open

### info[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GetAccountStateAPIResponseFormatted.html#info)

info: [AccountInfo](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.AccountInfo.html)

Metadata for the account which can be set with the [\['setInfo'\]](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html)
() method

#### Type declaration

Account info entry

*   ##### `Optional`defaultPermission?: [Permissions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Permissions.html)
    
    The default permissions for the account
    
*   ##### description: string
    
    A description for the account
    
*   ##### metadata: string
    
    Arbitrary metadata for the account
    
*   ##### `Optional`multisigQuorum?: bigint
    
    If this is a multisig account, the number of signers required
    
*   ##### name: string
    
    A name for the account
    
*   ##### `Optional`supply?: bigint
    
    If the account is a token, the supply of the token
    

### representative[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GetAccountStateAPIResponseFormatted.html#representative)

representative: [Account](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html)
 | null

The current representative for the account, if there is one this will be null if the account is not open or if the account has not yet set a representative

Properties
----------

### `Optional`account[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientConfig.html#account)

account?: [GenericAccount](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GenericAccount.html)

The account to use for the user client, if this is not supplied then the `signer` will be used (if available) otherwise this there will be no account and some operations may not be available.

### client[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientConfig.html#client)

client: [Client](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html)

The [Client](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Client.html)
 to use for interacting with the KeetaNet network

### `Optional`generateFeeBlock[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientConfig.html#generatefeeblock)

generateFeeBlock?: (  
    staple: [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
,  
    options?: [UserClientOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html)
,  
) \=> Promise<[Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
\>

Client provided function to compute the fee block associated with any transactions

### network[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientConfig.html#network)

network: bigint

The network to use for this instance of the [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html)

### networkAlias[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientConfig.html#networkalias)

networkAlias: "main" | "staging" | "test" | "dev"

The network alias to use for this instance of the [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html)

### `Optional`publishAidURL[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientConfig.html#publishaidurl)

publishAidURL?: string

If using the publish aid (see [\['usePublishAid'\]](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html)
) the URL to use.

The default depends on the network configuration.

### signer[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientConfig.html#signer)

signer: null | [Account](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html)
<[ECDSA\_SECP256K1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256k1)
 | [ED25519](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ed25519)
 | [ECDSA\_SECP256R1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256r1)
\>

Account to use to sign blocks for this instance of the [UserClient](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.UserClient.html)
 If this is `null` then no account will be used and some operations may not be available.

### `Optional`usePublishAid[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientConfig.html#usepublishaid)

usePublishAid?: boolean

Use the "publish aid" service to publish blocks.

This service handles requesting short votes and permanent votes and publishing to the network. This can reduce latency for clients far away from representatives because they only need to wait for a single response instead of multiple round-trips.

The default is false.

Class P2PSwitch
===============

A P2PSwitch is a method of coordinating messages from peers (either connected or known) with a Node.

### addManualPeer[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PSwitch.html#addmanualpeer)

*   addManualPeer(peer: [P2PPeer](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PPeer.html)
    , schedule?: boolean): void[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PSwitch.html#addmanualpeer-1)
    
    Add a manual peer to initiate peering process
    
    The switch will periodically reach out to this peer to build its peer database

    *   peer: [P2PPeer](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PPeer.html)
        
    *   schedule: boolean = true

### sendMessage[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PSwitch.html#sendmessage)

*   sendMessage(  
        to:  
            | null  
            | [Account](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html)
    <[ECDSA\_SECP256K1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256k1)
     | [ED25519](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ed25519)
     | [ECDSA\_SECP256R1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256r1)
    \>  
            | [P2PPeer](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PPeer.html)
      
            | [P2PConnection](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConnection.html)
    ,  
        id: string,  
        type: string,  
        data: any,  
        ttl?: number,  
        exclude?: (string | [P2PConnection](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConnection.html)
    )\[\],  
        skipConnectToPeers?: boolean,  
    ): Promise<boolean\>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PSwitch.html#sendmessage-1)
    
    Send a message to one or more peers
    
    If they are already connected that connection will be re-used, otherwise a new connection will be established.
    
    If "to" is null then it will be sent to a sampling of peers
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   to:  
            | null  
            | [Account](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Account.html)
        <[ECDSA\_SECP256K1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256k1)
         | [ED25519](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ed25519)
         | [ECDSA\_SECP256R1](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html#ecdsa_secp256r1)
        \>  
            | [P2PPeer](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PPeer.html)
          
            | [P2PConnection](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConnection.html)
        
        Where to send the message
        
    *   id: string
        
        Unique message identifier
        
    *   type: string
        
        Message type
        
    *   data: any
        
        Message data, must be serializable to JSON
        
    *   `Optional`ttl: number
        
        Maximum number of hops to pass through
        
    *   `Optional`exclude: (string | [P2PConnection](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConnection.html)
        )\[\]
        
        List of connections to exclude (e.g., because we got the message from that connection) if "to" is null
        
    *   skipConnectToPeers: boolean = false
        
        Skip creating new connections to send to, only connected clients will be sent the message
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<boolean\>
    
    If the message could be delivered to at least one peer
    

### stats[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PSwitch.html#stats)

# UserClientOptions | @keetanetwork/keetanet-client

*   [KeetaNetSDK](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.html)
    
*   [Referenced](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html)
    
*   [UserClientOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html)
    

Interface UserClientOptions
===========================

interface UserClientOptions {  
    [account](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html#account)
?: [GenericAccount](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GenericAccount.html)
;  
    [generateFeeBlock](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html#generatefeeblock)
?: (  
        staple: [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
,  
        options?: [UserClientOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html)
,  
    ) \=> Promise<[Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
\>;  
    [publishAidURL](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html#publishaidurl)
?: string;  
    [usePublishAid](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html#usepublishaid)
?: boolean;  
}

#### Hierarchy ([View Summary](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/hierarchy.html#KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions)
)

*   UserClientOptions
    *   [PublishOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PublishOptions.html)

### Properties

 [PgenerateFeeBlock?](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html#generatefeeblock)
 [PpublishAidURL?](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html#publishaidurl)
 [PusePublishAid?](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html#usepublishaid)

Properties
----------

### `Optional`account[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html#account)

account?: [GenericAccount](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GenericAccount.html)

The account to use for the user client, if this is not supplied then the `signer` will be used (if available) otherwise this there will be no account and some operations may not be available.

### `Optional`generateFeeBlock[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html#generatefeeblock)

generateFeeBlock?: (  
    staple: [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
,  
    options?: [UserClientOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html)
,  
) \=> Promise<[Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
\>

Client provided function to compute the fee block associated with any transactions

### `Optional`publishAidURL[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html#publishaidurl)

publishAidURL?: string

If using the publish aid (see [\['usePublishAid'\]](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html#)
) the URL to use.

The default depends on the network configuration.

### `Optional`usePublishAid[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html#usepublishaid)

usePublishAid?: boolean

Use the "publish aid" service to publish blocks.

This service handles requesting short votes and permanent votes and publishing to the network. This can reduce latency for clients far away from representatives because they only need to wait for a single response instead of multiple round-trips.

The default is false.

### Settings

*   External

### peers[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GetPeersAPIResponse.html#peers)

peers: [PeerInfo](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PeerInfo.html)
\[\]

The peers for the node

#### Type declaration

*   ##### `Optional`endpoint?: string
    
    If the peer is a representative and the peer has been greeted this will be the P2P endpoint for the representative
    
*   ##### `Optional`key?: string
    
    IF the peer is a representative and the peer has been greeted this will be the representative's public key
    
*   ##### kind: number
    
    The kind of peer that is connected, which is an index into the [NodeKind](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/enums/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.NodeKind.html)
     enum

# VoteBlockHash | @keetanetwork/keetanet-client

*   [KeetaNetSDK](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.html)
    
*   [Referenced](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.html)
    
*   [VoteBlockHash](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteBlockHash.html)
    

Class VoteBlockHash
===================

A VoteBlockHash is a hash of the blocks in a vote staple or vote staple it is a unique ID for the vote or vote staples contents regardless of which votes are included in the staple -- every vote in a vote staple has the same VoteBlockHash.

#### Hierarchy ([View Summary](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/hierarchy.html#KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteBlockHash)
)

Interface PrincipalACLWithInfoParsed
====================================

The response from the API when requesting the ACLs for a given account

interface PrincipalACLWithInfoParsed {  
    [balances](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PrincipalACLWithInfoParsed.html#balances)
: [GetAllBalancesResponse](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GetAllBalancesResponse.html)
;  
    [entity](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PrincipalACLWithInfoParsed.html#entity)
: [GenericAccount](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GenericAccount.html)
;  
    [info](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PrincipalACLWithInfoParsed.html#info)
: [AccountInfo](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.AccountInfo.html)
;  
    [principals](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PrincipalACLWithInfoParsed.html#principals)
: [ACLRow](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.ACLRow.html)
\[\];  
}

##### Index

### Properties

[Pbalances](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PrincipalACLWithInfoParsed.html#balances)
 [Pentity](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PrincipalACLWithInfoParsed.html#entity)
 [Pinfo](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PrincipalACLWithInfoParsed.html#info)

Properties
----------

### balances[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PrincipalACLWithInfoParsed.html#balances)

balances: [GetAllBalancesResponse](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GetAllBalancesResponse.html)

The balances for the `entity`

#### Type declaration

*   ##### balance: bigint
    
    The account balance of the specified token
    
    This is in raw units, and not the display units that the token uses
    
*   ##### token: [TokenAddress](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.TokenAddress.html)
    
    The account of the token
    

### entity[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PrincipalACLWithInfoParsed.html#entity)

entity: [GenericAccount](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GenericAccount.html)

The account for which this ACL is set

### info[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PrincipalACLWithInfoParsed.html#info)

info: [AccountInfo](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.AccountInfo.html)

Metadata for the `entity`

#### Type declaration

Account info entry

*   ##### `Optional`defaultPermission?: [Permissions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Permissions.html)
    
    The default permissions for the account
    
*   ##### description: string
    
    A description for the account
    
*   ##### metadata: string
    
    Arbitrary metadata for the account
    
*   ##### `Optional`multisigQuorum?: bigint
    
    If this is a multisig account, the number of signers required
    
*   ##### name: string
    
    A name for the account
    
*   ##### `Optional`supply?: bigint
    
    If the account is a token, the supply of the token
    

### principals[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PrincipalACLWithInfoParsed.html#principals)

principals: [ACLRow](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.ACLRow.html)
\[\]

#### Type declaration

Permissions types

*   ##### entity: [GenericAccount](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GenericAccount.html)
    
    The account that this row is for
    
*   ##### permissions: [Permissions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Permissions.html)
    
    The permissions for which this row grants
    
*   ##### principal: [GenericAccount](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GenericAccount.html)
    
    The account that these permissions apply to
    
*   ##### target: [GenericAccount](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GenericAccount.html)
    
    An optional target account which, depending on the permissions, may be targeted by the permission

Interface PublishOptions
========================

interface PublishOptions {  
    [account](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PublishOptions.html#account)
?: [GenericAccount](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GenericAccount.html)
;  
    [generateFeeBlock](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PublishOptions.html#generatefeeblock)
?: (  
        staple: [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
,  
        options?: [UserClientOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html)
,  
    ) \=> Promise<[Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
\>;  
    [publishAidURL](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PublishOptions.html#publishaidurl)
?: string;  
    [quotes](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PublishOptions.html#quotes)
?: [VoteQuote](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteQuote.html)
\[\];  
    [usePublishAid](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PublishOptions.html#usepublishaid)
?: boolean;  
}

#### Hierarchy ([View Summary](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/hierarchy.html#KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PublishOptions)
)

*   [UserClientOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html)
    *   PublishOptions

##### Index

### Properties

 [PgenerateFeeBlock?](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PublishOptions.html#generatefeeblock)
 [PpublishAidURL?](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PublishOptions.html#publishaidurl)

 [PusePublishAid?](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PublishOptions.html#usepublishaid)

Properties
----------

### `Optional`account[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PublishOptions.html#account)

account?: [GenericAccount](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GenericAccount.html)

The account to use for the user client, if this is not supplied then the `signer` will be used (if available) otherwise this there will be no account and some operations may not be available.

### `Optional`generateFeeBlock[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PublishOptions.html#generatefeeblock)

generateFeeBlock?: (  
    staple: [VoteStaple](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteStaple.html)
,  
    options?: [UserClientOptions](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html)
,  
) \=> Promise<[Block](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.Block.html)
\>

Client provided function to compute the fee block associated with any transactions

### `Optional`publishAidURL[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PublishOptions.html#publishaidurl)

publishAidURL?: string

If using the publish aid (see [\['usePublishAid'\]](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.UserClientOptions.html)
) the URL to use.

The default depends on the network configuration.

### `Optional`quotes[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PublishOptions.html#quotes)

quotes?: [VoteQuote](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.VoteQuote.html)
\[\]

Quotes if provided will be sent to the representative

### `Optional`usePublishAid[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.PublishOptions.html#usepublishaid)

usePublishAid?: boolean

Use the "publish aid" service to publish blocks.

This service handles requesting short votes and permanent votes and publishing to the network. This can reduce latency for clients far away from representatives because they only need to wait for a single response instead of multiple round-trips.

The default is false.

### Settings

### `Static`runTimer[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.RequestTiming.html#runtimer-2)

*   runTimer<[T](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.RequestTiming.html#runtimert-1)
    \>(  
        section: string,  
        timing: undefined | [RequestTiming](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.RequestTiming.html)
    ,  
        code: () \=> Promise<[T](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.RequestTiming.html#runtimert-1)
    \>,  
    ): Promise<[T](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.RequestTiming.html#runtimert-1)
    \>[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.RequestTiming.html#runtimer-3)
    
    Run a piece of code and time it, returning the result of the code
    
    This takes an optional "timing" parameter which is a timing object if one exists, to update -- if this parameter is undefined no timing will be done.
    
    #### Type Parameters
    
    *   T
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   section: string
        
        Name of the section to time -- should be unique within the code base so that it can be identified later
        
    *   timing: undefined | [RequestTiming](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.RequestTiming.html)
        
        Timing object to update, if available
        
    *   code: () \=> Promise<[T](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.RequestTiming.html#runtimert-1)
        \>
        
        The code to run
        
    
    #### Returns Promise<[T](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.RequestTiming.html#runtimert-1)
    \>
    
    The result of the code

Interface GetPeersOptions
=========================

Options for the "peers" method

interface GetPeersOptions {  
    [includeSelf](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GetPeersOptions.html#includeself)
?: boolean;  
    [includeStale](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GetPeersOptions.html#includestale)
?: boolean;  
    [includeUnverified](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GetPeersOptions.html#includeunverified)
?: boolean;  
}

##### Index

### Properties

[PincludeSelf?](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GetPeersOptions.html#includeself)
 [PincludeStale?](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GetPeersOptions.html#includestale)
 [PincludeUnverified?](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GetPeersOptions.html#includeunverified)

Properties
----------

### `Optional`includeSelf[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GetPeersOptions.html#includeself)

includeSelf?: boolean

Whether or not to consider ourselves (if we are attached to a representative node) as a peer. Default is false.

### `Optional`includeStale[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GetPeersOptions.html#includestale)

includeStale?: boolean

Whether or not to include peers which are potentially stale because we have not heard from them in a while. Default is false.

### `Optional`includeUnverified[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.GetPeersOptions.html#includeunverified)

includeUnverified?: boolean

Whether or not to include peers which have not been validated yet. Default is false.

### Settings

*   External

*   [KeetaNetSDK](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.html)

Class PublicKeyString<T>
========================

A "public key string" is a base32-encoded string with a prefix and checksum that contains the public key plus the algorithm information

#### Type Parameters

*   T extends [AccountKeyAlgorithm](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/enums/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.AccountKeyAlgorithm.html)

Interface BaseExternalKeyPairFunctions
======================================

Properties
----------

### `Optional`forwardingPeerCount[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConfig.html#forwardingpeercount)

forwardingPeerCount?: number

How many peers should messages that are being broadcast be forwarded to

### `Optional`kv[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConfig.html#kv)

kv?: null | [KVStorageProviderAPI](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.KVStorageProviderAPI.html)

Key-Value Storage Instance (null means a new in-memory instance will be created)

### `Optional`manualPeersCheckInFreq[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConfig.html#manualpeerscheckinfreq)

manualPeersCheckInFreq?: number

How frequently should manual peers be connected to in order to get additional peering information (ms)

### `Optional`seenMessageTTL[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConfig.html#seenmessagettl)

seenMessageTTL?: number

Forget about seen message ids after this many milliseconds

### `Optional`timeoutIdle[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConfig.html#timeoutidle)

timeoutIdle?: number

How long should a P2P connection allowed to be idle (after greeting, excluding listeners) (ms)

### `Optional`timeoutIdleCheck[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConfig.html#timeoutidlecheck)

timeoutIdleCheck?: number

How frequently should the idle connection checker run and terminate connections (ms)

### `Optional`timeoutIdleGreeting[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConfig.html#timeoutidlegreeting)

timeoutIdleGreeting?: number

How long should a P2P connection be allowed to be idle for a greeting to complete (ms)

### `Optional`timeoutIdleListener[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConfig.html#timeoutidlelistener)

timeoutIdleListener?: number

How long should a P2P connection from a listener allowed to be idle (ms)

### `Optional`useHTTPRepublish[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConfig.html#usehttprepublish)

useHTTPRepublish?: boolean

Whether to republish over http or p2p websockets

=========================

Options for signing and verifying

interface signOptionsType {  
    [forCert](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.signOptionsType.html#forcert)
?: boolean;  
    [raw](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.signOptionsType.html#raw)
?: boolean;  
}

##### Index

### Properties

[PforCert?](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.signOptionsType.html#forcert)

Properties
----------

### `Optional`forCert[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.signOptionsType.html#forcert)

forCert?: boolean

Is this signing or verification for an X.509 certificate ? The format can be different between our native format and what is accept for X.509

### `Optional`raw[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.signOptionsType.html#raw)

raw?: boolean

Perform signing or verification on the raw data ?

The default is false (meaning the data will get hashed before signed)

# P2PConnection | @keetanetwork/keetanet-client

*   [KeetaNetSDK](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/modules/KeetaNetSDK.html)

Interface P2PConnection
=======================

Represents an active connection on the P2P network

interface P2PConnection {  
    [abort](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConnection.html#abort)
: boolean;  
    [close](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConnection.html#close)
: () \=> Promise<void\>;  
    [connString](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConnection.html#connstring)
: string;  
    [peer](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConnection.html#peer)
: null | [P2PPeer](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PPeer.html)
;  
    [peerString](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConnection.html#peerstring)
: null | string;  
    [send](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConnection.html#send)
: (data: Buffer) \=> Promise<boolean\>;  
    [serialize](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConnection.html#serialize)
?: () \=> [SerializedConnection](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.SerializedConnection.html)
;  
    [timeout](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConnection.html#timeout)
: number;  
    [validatedPeer](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConnection.html#validatedpeer)
: null | [P2PPeer](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PPeer.html)
;  
}

##### Index

### Properties

[Pabort](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConnection.html#abort)
 [Pclose](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConnection.html#close)

Properties
----------

### abort[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConnection.html#abort)

abort: boolean

Signal to abort the connection

### close[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConnection.html#close)

close: () \=> Promise<void\>

Method to close this connection

### connString[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConnection.html#connstring)

connString: string

Printable form of the connection

### peer[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConnection.html#peer)

peer: null | [P2PPeer](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PPeer.html)

Peer information, if null the peer has not yet greeted us so we do not know who they are

### peerString[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConnection.html#peerstring)

peerString: null | string

Printable form of the peer

### send[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConnection.html#send)

send: (data: Buffer) \=> Promise<boolean\>

Method to send a message to this peer

### `Optional`serialize[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConnection.html#serialize)

serialize?: () \=> [SerializedConnection](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.SerializedConnection.html)

If this connection can be serialized to our KV store, return the type.

See [P2PSwitch.RegisterP2PConnectionDeserializer](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/classes/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PSwitch.html#registerp2pconnectiondeserializer)
 for registering a handler to convert it back

### timeout[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConnection.html#timeout)

timeout: number

Timeout of when to close this connection (Date.valueOf)

### validatedPeer[](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/interfaces/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PConnection.html#validatedpeer)

validatedPeer: null | [P2PPeer](https://static.network.keeta.com/docs/types/KeetaNetSDK.Referenced.P2PPeer.html)

The peering information is normally untrusted since it just what the peer gave us once it has been validated we can populate this.

Validation can be via a callback to the peer or a certificate

A callback isn't as reliable since it could be a MITM proxy
